root_text.an2.130:1.1: “Saddho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ sammā āyācamāno āyāceyya:
root_text.an2.130:1.3: Esā, bhikkhave, tulā etaṁ pamāṇaṁ mama sāvakānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ yadidaṁ sāriputtamoggallānā”ti.
root_text.an2.131:1.1: “Saddhā, bhikkhave, bhikkhunī evaṁ sammā āyācamānā āyāceyya:
root_text.an2.131:1.2: ‘tādisī homi yādisī khemā ca bhikkhunī uppalavaṇṇā cā’ti.
root_text.an2.131:1.3: Esā, bhikkhave, tulā etaṁ pamāṇaṁ mama sāvikānaṁ bhikkhunīnaṁ yadidaṁ khemā ca bhikkhunī uppalavaṇṇā cā”ti.
root_text.an2.132:1.1: “Saddho, bhikkhave, upāsako evaṁ sammā āyācamāno āyāceyya:
root_text.an2.132:1.2: ‘tādiso homi yādiso citto ca gahapati hatthako ca āḷavako’ti.
root_text.an2.132:1.3: Esā, bhikkhave, tulā etaṁ pamāṇaṁ mama sāvakānaṁ upāsakānaṁ yadidaṁ citto ca gahapati hatthako ca āḷavako”ti.
root_text.an2.133:1.1: “Saddhā, bhikkhave, upāsikā evaṁ sammā āyācamānā āyāceyya:
root_text.an2.133:1.2: ‘tādisī homi yādisī khujjuttarā ca upāsikā veḷukaṇḍakiyā ca nandamātā’ti.
root_text.an2.133:1.3: Esā, bhikkhave, tulā etaṁ pamāṇaṁ mama sāvikānaṁ upāsikānaṁ yadidaṁ khujjuttarā ca upāsikā veḷukaṇḍakiyā ca nandamātā”ti.
root_text.an2.134:1.1: “Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.134:1.5: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavatīti.
root_text.an2.134:2.1: Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.134:2.5: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavatī”ti.
root_text.an2.135:1.1: “Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.135:1.3: Ananuvicca apariyogāhetvā appasādanīye ṭhāne pasādaṁ upadaṁseti,
root_text.an2.135:1.4: ananuvicca apariyogāhetvā pasādanīye ṭhāne appasādaṁ upadaṁseti.
root_text.an2.135:1.5: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavatīti.
root_text.an2.135:2.1: Dvīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.135:2.3: Anuvicca pariyogāhetvā appasādanīye ṭhāne appasādaṁ upadaṁseti,
root_text.an2.135:2.4: anuvicca pariyogāhetvā pasādanīye ṭhāne pasādaṁ upadaṁseti.
root_text.an2.135:2.5: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dvīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavatī”ti.
root_text.an2.136:1.1: “Dvīsu, bhikkhave, micchāpaṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.136:1.4: Imesu kho, bhikkhave, dvīsu micchāpaṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavatīti.
root_text.an2.136:2.1: Dvīsu, bhikkhave, sammāpaṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.136:2.4: Imesu kho, bhikkhave, dvīsu sammāpaṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavatī”ti.
root_text.an2.137:1.1: “Dvīsu, bhikkhave, micchāpaṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.137:1.4: Imesu kho, bhikkhave, micchāpaṭipajjamāno bālo abyatto asappuriso khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavatīti.
root_text.an2.137:2.1: Dvīsu, bhikkhave, sammāpaṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an2.137:2.4: Imesu kho, bhikkhave, dvīsu sammāpaṭipajjamāno paṇḍito viyatto sappuriso akkhataṁ anupahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, anavajjo ca hoti ananuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca puññaṁ pasavatī”ti.
root_text.an2.138:1.3: Sacittavodānañca na ca kiñci loke upādiyati.

translation_text.an2.130:1.2: ‘May I be like Sāriputta and Moggallāna!’
translation_text.an2.130:1.3: These are a standard and a measure for my monk disciples, that is, Sāriputta and Moggallāna.”
translation_text.an2.131:1.2: ‘May I be like the nuns Khemā and Uppalavaṇṇā!’
translation_text.an2.131:1.3: These are a standard and a measure for my nun disciples, that is, the nuns Khemā and Uppalavaṇṇā.”
translation_text.an2.132:1.2: ‘May I be like the householder Citta and Hatthaka of Āḷavī!’
translation_text.an2.132:1.3: These are a standard and a measure for my male lay followers, that is, the householder Citta and Hatthaka of Āḷavī.”
translation_text.an2.133:1.2: ‘May I be like the laywomen Khujjuttarā and Veḷukaṇṭakī, Nanda’s mother!’
translation_text.an2.133:1.3: These are a standard and a measure for my female lay disciples, that is, the laywomen Khujjuttarā and Veḷukaṇṭakī, Nanda’s mother.”
translation_text.an2.134:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person has two qualities they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.134:1.3: Without examining or scrutinizing, they praise those deserving of criticism
translation_text.an2.134:1.4: and they criticize those deserving of praise.
translation_text.an2.134:1.5: When a foolish, incompetent untrue person possesses these two qualities they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they create much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.134:2.1: When an astute, competent true person has two qualities they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an2.134:2.3: After examining and scrutinizing, they criticize those deserving of criticism
translation_text.an2.134:2.4: and they praise those deserving of praise.
translation_text.an2.134:2.5: When an astute, competent true person possesses these two qualities they keep themselves healthy and whole. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they create much merit.”
translation_text.an2.135:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person has two qualities they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.135:1.3: Without examining or scrutinizing, they arouse faith in things that are dubious,
translation_text.an2.135:1.4: and they don’t arouse faith in things that are inspiring.
translation_text.an2.135:1.5: When a foolish, incompetent untrue person possesses these two qualities they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they create much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.135:2.1: When an astute, competent true person has two qualities they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an2.135:2.3: After examining or scrutinizing, they don’t arouse faith in things that are dubious,
translation_text.an2.135:2.4: and they do arouse faith in things that are inspiring.
translation_text.an2.135:2.5: When an astute, competent true person possesses these two qualities they keep themselves healthy and whole. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they create much merit.”
translation_text.an2.136:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person acts wrongly toward two individuals they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.136:1.4: When a foolish, incompetent untrue person acts wrongly toward these two individuals they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.136:2.1: When an astute, competent true person acts rightly toward two individuals they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an2.136:2.4: When an astute, competent true person acts rightly toward these two individuals they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.”
translation_text.an2.137:1.1: “When a foolish, incompetent untrue person acts wrongly toward two individuals they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.137:1.3: The Realized One and a disciple of the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.137:1.4: When a foolish, incompetent untrue person acts wrongly toward these people they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an2.137:2.1: When an astute, competent true person acts rightly toward two individuals they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.
translation_text.an2.137:2.3: The Realized One and a disciple of the Realized One.
translation_text.an2.137:2.4: When an astute, competent true person acts rightly toward these two individuals they keep themselves intact and unscathed. They don’t deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much merit.”
translation_text.an2.138:1.3: Cleaning your own mind, and not grasping at anything in the world.

reference_text.an2.133:1.1: csp1ed15.88, csp2ed15.88, ms15A2_220, msdiv134, ndp16.82, pts-vp-pli1.89.1
